_doc: The single home where every product launch lives. Add a product here, run scripts/launch-organ.py --stage --apply to generate channel-native drafts under staged/<product>/<channel>.md, refine them, then fire by hand (the organ never sends). Briefs must be DISCOVERED facts (real version/proof/links), never fabricated weight. This file PUBLISHES nothing private — public product URLs only.

channels.label: Hacker News — Show HN
channels.note: Title: 'Show HN: <name> — <one-liner>'. One factual first comment: what it is, why you built it, the tech, the link. HN punishes marketing tone — no adjectives, no hype.

channels.label: Reddit
channels.note: Choose the subreddit per product and read its self-promotion rules first. Lead with the problem, disclose you built it, be a participant not an ad.

channels.label: Product Hunt
channels.note: Tagline <= 60 chars + a short description + the maker's first comment. Schedule for 00:01 PT to get a full day on the leaderboard.

products.name: ChatGPT Exporter
products.one_liner: export your ChatGPT history to Markdown, HTML, JSON, PNG, or plain text — browser-only, no account
products.audience: researchers, writers, and developers who keep real work inside ChatGPT and need it out at the format level
products.proof: live userscript v2.29.1, localized in 9 languages, free with an optional Pro tier (bulk/all-conversation export, conversations.json import/re-export); open source, no backend, no cloud upload
products.why_built: once conversations are in the ChatGPT web UI they are hard to move into the systems you already use; the official JSON export is not the same as archiving, citing, searching, and repurposing at the format you need
